meta.strength_semantics: strength_score represents curation confidence and semantic transmission prominence, not causal effect size.
meta.notes: Curated relation layer for Chart 02 semantic transmission machine.

evidence_registry.label: WHO depression fact sheet
evidence_registry.citation_note: Supports symptoms, treatment, stigma, social functioning, and health-system response.

evidence_registry.label: NIMH depression publication
evidence_registry.citation_note: Supports symptoms, diagnosis, daily functioning, treatment, and help-seeking.

evidence_registry.label: CDC/NCHS Data Brief 527
evidence_registry.citation_note: Supports PHQ-based prevalence, functional difficulty, and treatment uptake.

evidence_registry.label: NHANES depression screener / DPQ documentation
evidence_registry.citation_note: Supports PHQ-9 screening and functional-difficulty measurement.

evidence_registry.label: NLM MeSH Depression
evidence_registry.citation_note: Supports depression as an affective/emotional vocabulary entry.

evidence_registry.label: NLM MeSH Depressive Disorder
evidence_registry.citation_note: Supports depressive disorder as a mood-disorder classification.

evidence_registry.label: NLM MeSH Major Depressive Disorder
evidence_registry.citation_note: Supports major depressive disorder, entry terms, qualifiers, and indexing.

evidence_registry.label: Clement et al. systematic review on stigma and help-seeking
evidence_registry.citation_note: Supports stigma as a drag force on help-seeking.

evidence_registry.label: Gulliver et al. review on perceived barriers and facilitators to help-seeking
evidence_registry.citation_note: Supports stigma, embarrassment, poor symptom recognition, and self-reliance as barriers.

evidence_registry.label: Help-seeking process model for untreated depressive symptoms
evidence_registry.citation_note: Supports symptoms to self-identification to intention to help-seeking behavior.

evidence_registry.label: WHO mental health and media guidance
evidence_registry.citation_note: Supports media as a coupling layer for stigma, awareness, and help-seeking.

relations.short_explanation: Controlled vocabulary separates depression as affective description from depressive disorder and major depressive disorder as clinical classifications.
relations.suggested_visual_treatment: split clutch with two unequal lanes
relations.suggested_motion_behaviour: initial pulse divides into a softer affective channel and a firmer diagnostic channel

relations.short_explanation: Indexing bundles entry terms, synonyms, and public labels around clinical names, mediating movement between literature retrieval, diagnosis, and everyday wording.
relations.suggested_motion_behaviour: small alias gears periodically engage the main indexing gear

relations.short_explanation: Medical indexing fans outward across diagnosis, drug therapy, therapy, psychology, and epidemiology.
relations.suggested_visual_treatment: fan-out manifold with fine toothed belts
relations.suggested_motion_behaviour: steady indexing rotation distributes low-amplitude motion into five subchannels

relations.short_explanation: Symptom clusters and impairment act as the gate through which distress becomes clinically named.
relations.suggested_motion_behaviour: target rotates only after multiple symptom inputs accumulate beyond threshold

relations.short_explanation: Behavioral expressions shape how people recognize, describe, and respond to depressive experience.
relations.suggested_motion_behaviour: continuous low-level exchange rather than hard gear teeth

relations.short_explanation: Diagnosis opens multiple care pathways, including medication and counseling or therapy.
relations.suggested_motion_behaviour: one clinical rotation branches into two parallel care tracks

relations.short_explanation: PHQ-style screening translates depressive symptoms into repeated, scored questionnaire items.
relations.suggested_motion_behaviour: each symptom adds a small indexed tick that accumulates into a score wheel

relations.short_explanation: Functional difficulty is built into the screening regime rather than sitting outside it.
relations.suggested_visual_treatment: sidecar gauge attached to the screening gear
relations.suggested_motion_behaviour: every score pulse also nudges an impairment needle

relations.short_explanation: Survey screening, public-health records, and diagnosis organize depression through different measurement regimes.
relations.suggested_visual_treatment: translator clutch between survey and clinical clusters
relations.suggested_motion_behaviour: intermittent ratchet converts one metric regime into another without full synchronization

relations.short_explanation: Population records turn screening and survey data into prevalence patterns that become visible to institutions and publics.
relations.suggested_visual_treatment: histogram belt feeding a larger reporting gear
relations.suggested_motion_behaviour: many small record pulses aggregate into periodic public-facing surges

relations.short_explanation: Functional difficulty mediates whether symptoms become more visible, clinically legible, and connected to care.
relations.suggested_motion_behaviour: impedance drops as impairment rises, increasing downstream motion

relations.short_explanation: Awareness and literacy help symptoms become recognized, named, and connected to possible care.
relations.suggested_visual_treatment: bridge gear crossing from discourse to care
relations.suggested_motion_behaviour: awareness pulses first increase self-recognition, then travel outward toward disclosure and help-seeking

relations.short_explanation: Stigma acts as a drag force on acknowledgment, disclosure, and help-seeking.
relations.suggested_motion_behaviour: adds resistance, lag, and partial motion loss to outgoing care pathways

relations.short_explanation: Symptoms do not automatically become named illness; they pass through a recognition layer.
relations.suggested_motion_behaviour: several soft inputs must align before the self-description gear locks into place

relations.short_explanation: Disclosure conditions whether social support, formal care, and anti-stigma systems can operate.
relations.suggested_motion_behaviour: opening increases flow to support nodes; secrecy keeps the valve partly closed

relations.short_explanation: Family, friends, schools, public training, and social support can facilitate or inhibit movement toward formal help.
relations.suggested_motion_behaviour: relation can amplify or dampen depending on local state; never a simple one-way push

relations.short_explanation: Treatment should not be a terminal sink; visible care and prior experience can re-enter awareness, stigma, and future help-seeking.
relations.suggested_motion_behaviour: downstream care nodes send slower, damped pulses back toward recognition and access layers

relations.short_explanation: Media discourse can support awareness and help-seeking language, but can also reinforce stigma or stereotypes.
relations.suggested_visual_treatment: wide coupling belt with positive and negative teeth
relations.suggested_motion_behaviour: bidirectional oscillation whose tone changes amplitude sent to awareness versus stigma

relations.short_explanation: Public discourse shapes the socially available phrases people can use to describe themselves.
relations.suggested_visual_treatment: discourse ribbon feeding a label wheel
relations.suggested_motion_behaviour: repeated public phrases leave small accumulated turns on self-description and disclosure nodes

relations.short_explanation: Public programmes and anti-stigma outreach can act as relation-makers for awareness, disclosure comfort, and help-seeking.
relations.suggested_motion_behaviour: policy pulses split into outreach, disclosure comfort, and support behavior channels

relations.short_explanation: Prevalence reports recirculate into policy, public discourse, and awareness as a soft institutional feedback loop.
relations.suggested_visual_treatment: delayed reporting loop with public relay gear
relations.suggested_motion_behaviour: periodic batch release after measurement cycles; strong delay and damping
